title: To provide technical and financial assistance for groundwater recharge, aquifer storage, and water source substitution projects.
plain_summary: This bill lets the government use leftover money from a big infrastructure law to help with water projects in the western United States. The money can be used to help communities store water underground, put water into underground storage areas, or switch to different water sources to protect underground water supplies. Up to three million dollars per year can be used for these projects from 2026 to 2031. The goal is to help western communities better manage their water supply.
last_action_text: Referred to the House Committee on Natural Resources.

sections.title: Section 1  Assistance for Groundwater Recharge, Aquifer Storage, and Water Source Substitution Projects
sections.text: This section changes an existing law called the Infrastructure Investment and Jobs Act. It adds new rules about money that can be used for water projects. The Secretary can use three million dollars each year from twenty twenty six through twenty thirty one. This money comes from funds that were already approved but not yet spent under section forty thousand nine hundred and one. The money must be used for projects that help put water back into underground water supplies, store water underground, or help communities switch to different water sources.
